signals.headline: Earlier Easter announcements (April 9-10) do not qualify as binding or sustained, per historical patterns of violations
signals.raw:
Earlier Easter announcements (April 9-10) do not qualify as binding or sustained, per historical patterns of violations.[1][3][4]

No mentions of "Ternuvate" (possibly a misspelling, e.g., Ternopil or similar) or fresh Russia-related confirmations in April 2026 news.[1-6] Ongoing talks (e.g., Putin’s June 2024 terms) remain unresolved.[6]

signals.headline: **US annual core inflation** hit 3
signals.raw: **US annual core inflation** hit 3.9% (pre-oil shock), pushing 3-month annualized core PCE to 4.56%—over double the Fed's 2% target—and has exceeded target for five years, with oil above $100/barrel signaling further rises[1].

signals.headline: **Kevin Warsh** set to succeed Jerome Powell by mid-May 2026; Powell's final meeting was Wednesday (April 15), leaving W
signals.raw: **Kevin Warsh** set to succeed Jerome Powell by mid-May 2026; Powell's final meeting was Wednesday (April 15), leaving Warsh to face immediate rate decisions amid Middle East energy shock[1].

signals.headline: Fed held rates at 3
signals.raw: Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75%, projecting one 25bp cut this year, but 7/19 officials expect no cuts, traders doubt easing, and officials discuss hikes if pressures persist[1].
